name: Yttria-Stabilized Zirconia
description: Yttria-Stabilized Zirconia is a technical ceramic suitable for laser cleaning applications.

laserReflectivity.sourceName: Optical Properties of Yttria-Stabilized Zirconia Ceramics for Laser Applications

laserPower.description: Optimal average power for ceramic cleaning

spotSize.description: Beam diameter range for precise vs. broad cleaning; 200 μm ideal for YSZ industrial applications like coating prep to focus energy without substrate etching. Source: Equipment specifications from Trumpf and CleanLaser systems for ceramic processing.

frequency.description: Standard for pulsed cleaning to ensure pulse overlap without excessive heat accumulation on low-conductivity YSZ; higher rates speed up process but risk thermal buildup. Source: Academic literature in Applied Surface Science (review on pulse repetition effects, 2019).

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for industrial YSZ cleaning to minimize heat-affected zone (HAZ) due to YSZ's high melting point (~2700°C) and moderate reflectivity at 1064 nm; picosecond (ps) or femtosecond (fs) for ultra-precise but costlier. Source: Laser physics research in Optics Express (e.g., study on ceramic ablation thresholds, 2018).

scanSpeed.description: Effective range for 1064 nm cleaning, slower for heavy contamination (e.g., oxides) and faster for light; 500 mm/s recommended for optimal fluence delivery on YSZ without overheating. Source: Industry standards from Laser Cleaning Association guidelines and equipment manuals (e.g., Adapt Laser Systems).

passCount.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass for heavy; 2 passes recommended for complete YSZ cleaning while monitoring for microcracks. Source: Industry case studies in Surface and Coatings Technology (YSZ thermal barrier cleaning, 2021).

overlapRatio.description: Ensures uniform coverage without gaps or over-ablation on brittle YSZ; 50% balances efficiency and even energy distribution. Source: Process optimization studies in Lasers in Manufacturing and Materials Processing (scan strategy for ceramics, 2022).
